name: Democratic People's Republic of Korea — Strategic Leadership KPV Assessment (Open Source)
description: Open-source KPV assessment of DPRK strategic leadership. AO INVERTED. DPRK is the most opaque major adversarial architecture; many AO scores are low (1.0-1.5) indicating significant allied intelligence gaps. All data from public sources: 38 North, KIDA, RAND, IISS, NTI, CRS.
ao_note: INVERTED AO. DPRK is the most opaque major adversarial org — low AO scores indicate significant intelligence gaps that are themselves strategic findings.

roles.title: Kim Jong Un — Supreme Leader / WPK General Secretary / SAC Chairman
roles.notes: Most hypercentralised adversarial architecture. Kim Yo Jong (sister) and Kim Jong Chol (brother) as potential successors; Kim Ju Ae (daughter) being publicly positioned. Succession architecture is informal and opaque. Source: 38 North; KIDA; CRS R41438.

roles.title: Kim Yo Jong — First Deputy Director, WPK Propaganda
roles.notes: Publicly identified; substantive policy authority documented through public statements. Second most powerful individual in DPRK by open-source analysis. Source: 38 North; NK News; CRS R41438.

roles.title: Director, Reconnaissance General Bureau
roles.notes: INTELLIGENCE GAP: AO=2.0. RGB controls Lazarus Group (Unit 121) cyber, foreign currency operations, and HUMINT. Director identity uncertain/disputed in open sources. Source: UN Panel of Experts reports; CISA AA22-108A; Mandiant UNC4899.

roles.title: RGB Unit 121 — Cyber Operations Director (Lazarus Group)
roles.notes: CRITICAL INTELLIGENCE GAP: AO=1.0. Lazarus Group leadership is the most opaque cyber operational node in any major adversarial architecture. Crypto theft ($3B+ 2021-2023) funds WMD programme. Source: UN 1718 Committee reports; US-CERT AA22-108A; CISA advisories.

roles.title: Chief Nuclear Weapons Designer — NWRC / Yongbyon
roles.notes: CRITICAL INTELLIGENCE GAP: AO=1.0. The single most consequential intelligence gap in adversarial KPV analysis. DPRK warhead design leadership is essentially unknown to allied open-source analysts. 30-60 weapons estimated. Source: 38 North Yongbyon analysis; SIPRI DPRK estimate; NTI DPRK.

roles.title: ICBM Programme Director — Hwasong-17/18 Development
roles.notes: INTELLIGENCE GAP: AO=1.5. Hwasong-17 (liquid ICBM) and Hwasong-18 (solid ICBM) programme directors are unknown. KCNA announces test events but technical leadership is opaque. Source: KCNA public test announcements; 38 North imagery analysis; RAND PE-388.

roles.title: WPK Organisation and Guidance Department Director
roles.notes: OGD controls party discipline and surveillance over military/security services. Historically most powerful node beneath Supreme Leader. AO=2.0 — leadership identity uncertain. Source: Lankov; KIDA; 38 North political analysis.

roles.title: Office 39 Director — Foreign Currency Operations
roles.notes: WMD and regime financing through sanctions evasion; crypto theft coordination with Unit 121. Source: UN 1718 Committee reports; Treasury OFAC SDN (entity-level); CRS R41438.
